auth_notes: Requires creating an App Registration in the Azure Portal, configuring Redirect URIs, and granting specific Microsoft Graph API permissions (e.g., Sites.Read.All, Files.ReadWrite.All). Admin consent is often required for these scopes.
rate_limits: Throttling occurs based on request volume per tenant; typically limits are around 10,000 requests per 10 minutes, but vary by service and license.
gotchas: Files larger than 4MB require a multi-step 'Upload Session' API flow rather than a single POST request.
SharePoint Site IDs and Drive IDs are not user-friendly and must be retrieved via search or list endpoints before performing file operations.
The Microsoft Graph API is the modern standard, but some legacy environments still require the older SharePoint REST API (v1), which has different auth requirements.
client_must_provide: Microsoft Entra Admin Access (for initial app consent)
operations_available: List items in a SharePoint List
Upload and Download files to Document Libraries
research_notes: The integration should be built using the HTTP Request node targeting the Microsoft Graph API (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0). Developers must configure n8n OAuth2 credentials using the Microsoft Auth and Token endpoints, ensuring the 'Sites' and 'Files' scopes are correctly mapped to the Azure App Registration.

effort_recommendation.rationale: The integration should be built using the HTTP Request node targeting the Microsoft Graph API (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0). Developers must configure n8n OAuth2 credentials using the Microsoft Auth and Token endpoints, ensuring the 'Sites' and 'Files' scopes are correctly mapped to the Azure App Registration.
